label: Dry cracked forearms without infection or systemic skin-disease red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of dry, cracked forearms skin or forearms xerosis.
No skin infection, deep open wound, severe cutaneous reaction, systemic illness, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Skin infection, cellulitis, abscess, deep open wound, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin peeling or sloughing, mucosal lesions, systemic illness, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/wound plan.
must_not_miss: Systemic skin disease flare requiring separate care.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports dry-skin self-care with gentle skin care, moisturizers, avoiding irritants, and escalation for infection, severe rash, or systemic symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized dry cracked skin with infection, deep wound, severe-rash, systemic, pregnancy/pediatric, immunocompromised, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports dry-skin self-care with gentle skin care, moisturizers, avoiding irritants, and escalation for infection, severe rash, or systemic symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized dry cracked skin with infection, deep wound, severe-rash, systemic, pregnancy/pediatric, immunocompromised, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of dry cracked forearms or forearms xerosis
no infection or deep open wound
no systemic or mucosal red flags
